data.label: Backup Power Unit
data.description: Self-contained subsystem used by parent RBD analyses

assumptions: Blocks are nonrepairable during the modeled mission.

data.label: System Failure
data.description: Loss of the required pumping function

data.label: Loss of Function
data.description: Either failure mode causes the top event

data.label: Unsafe Ordered Failure
data.description: Protection must fail before the demand occurs

blocks.name: Controller Board Assembly
blocks.notes: Representative 75% operating / 25% nonoperating exposure. Operating rates use MIL-HDBK-217F; nonoperating rates use the selected RADC-TR-85-91 models.

hazards.title: Loss of required pumping
hazards.description: The controller cannot sustain required flow during the mission.
hazards.cause: Power interruption or undetected sensor bias
hazards.mitigation: Power health monitor and sensor comparison

requirements.statement: The controller shall complete the 1,000-hour mission without loss of pumping with reliability at least 0.99 at 90% confidence.
requirements.failureDefinition: Loss of required flow for more than 2 seconds

rcm.rationale: Inspect contact-resistance trend until field evidence supports an interval update.

planning.subject: Pump controller
planning.scope: Electrical power input through commanded pump output
planning.intent: Prevent loss or degradation of commanded pumping
planning.tasks: Design review, analysis, verification, action closure
planning.tools: System requirements, hazard log, FRACAS, verification evidence
planning.assumptions: Nominal 1,000-hour mission and declared electrical interfaces

structure_nodes.name: Pump control system
structure_nodes.interface: Power, sensor, actuator, and operator interfaces

structure_nodes.name: Pump controller
structure_nodes.description: Processes commands and controls the pump
structure_nodes.interface: 28 VDC input and pump drive output

structure_nodes.name: Power connector
structure_nodes.description: Connects external power to the controller

functions.notes: Maintains the controller power domain through the external connector.
Provides the feedback signal used by the pump-control loop.
Focus-element function allocated from the system requirement.

function_links.label: supplies power
function_links.rationale: The control function requires uninterrupted controller power.

function_links.label: pressure feedback
function_links.rationale: Discharge-pressure feedback closes the control loop.

functional_requirements.statement: The power path shall deliver controller power without an interruption longer than 20 ms.
functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: No interruption longer than 20 ms and contact resistance below 20 mΩ.
functional_requirements.operating_condition: Startup and normal pumping over the qualified environment
functional_requirements.verification_method: Continuity monitoring during vibration and thermal testing

functional_requirements.statement: The pressure interface shall report discharge pressure within ±2% of full scale.
functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Absolute error does not exceed 2% of full scale.
functional_requirements.operating_condition: Normal pumping over the qualified temperature range

functional_requirements.statement: The controller shall maintain the commanded pumping function for the 1,000-hour mission.
functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: No loss of required flow for more than 2 seconds.

interfaces.name: Controller power input
interfaces.flow_description: Qualified DC power enters the controller through the mated connector.

interfaces.name: Pressure feedback
interfaces.flow_description: Conditioned pressure feedback is delivered to the control function.

items.label: 28 VDC bus
items.description: External supply applied to the connector.

items.label: Continuous controller power
items.description: Power delivered within the interruption and resistance limits.

items.label: Contact force
items.description: Specified contact geometry, finish, and retention.

items.label: Vibration and temperature
items.description: Environmental loads that promote fretting and resistance change.

items.label: Intermittent open circuit
items.description: Output power is interrupted beyond the allowable duration.

failure_chains.cause: Fretting corrosion at the connector contacts
failure_chains.prevention_controls: Qualified contact finish and connector retention
failure_chains.detection_controls: Design review and vibration functional test
failure_chains.severity_rationale: Sustained interruption removes the required system function
failure_chains.occurrence_rationale: Relevant connector history supports a low but credible occurrence
failure_chains.detection_rationale: Intermittency can escape static continuity inspection

actions.description: Add contact-force margin and strain-relief verification

failure_chains.remarks: Initial and post-action priority reviewed with the design team.

failure_chains.cause: Sensor offset drifts with accumulated exposure
failure_chains.occurrence_rationale: Drift remains possible within the selected design
failure_chains.detection_rationale: A single-channel plausibility check has moderate coverage

planning.subject: Power connector assembly
planning.tasks: Process review and Control Plan alignment
planning.tools: Work instruction, gage study, Control Plan
planning.assumptions: Approved connector and calibrated insertion-force monitor

structure_nodes.name: Seat power connector
structure_nodes.description: Insert connector to the positive latch

structure_nodes.name: Confirm latch and retention
structure_nodes.description: Verify latch position and pull retention

functions.description: Fully seat and retain the power connector
functions.notes: Process-step function controlled by insertion travel and retention verification.

functional_requirements.statement: The assembly process shall fully engage the connector latch and achieve at least 35 N retention force.
functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Vision confirms latch position and monitored pull force is at least 35 N.
functional_requirements.verification_method: Vision inspection and monitored pull test

interfaces.name: Operator insertion input
interfaces.flow_description: The operator and fixture apply insertion force and travel.

interfaces.name: Completed connector output
interfaces.flow_description: A latched, retained connector leaves the process step.

items.label: Connector and insertion command
items.description: Approved parts presented to the seating operation.

items.label: Latched connector
items.description: Connector is fully seated and retained.

items.label: Fixture travel interlock
items.description: Controlled insertion travel and force limits.

items.label: Incomplete seating
items.description: Connector appears installed without positive latch engagement.

failure_chains.effect: Controller power becomes intermittent in service
failure_chains.cause: Insertion is stopped before positive latch engagement
failure_chains.detection_controls: Latch vision check and monitored pull test
failure_chains.severity_rationale: The downstream system can lose its required function
failure_chains.occurrence_rationale: Manual insertion retains a credible incomplete-seat cause
failure_chains.detection_rationale: Two controls exist but the combined escape rate is not yet demonstrated

actions.description: Interlock the fixture on measured latch travel

failure_chains.remarks: Control Plan alignment remains open pending fixture interlock verification.

control_plan.specification: Latch position present; pull force at least 35 N
control_plan.reaction_plan: Stop the station, contain since last accepted check, and correct the fixture or work method

planning.subject: In-service connector monitoring and response
planning.scope: Detect power-path degradation and transition to the safe state
planning.exclusions: Facility power loss outside the controller boundary
planning.intent: Evaluate diagnostic monitoring during customer operation
planning.assumptions: Health monitor is active throughout the stated mission

functions.notes: Monitors the power-path voltage drop during customer operation.
Removes the pump command and annunciates maintenance before the fault-tolerant interval expires.

function_links.label: fault indication
function_links.rationale: Detection initiates the declared system response.

functional_requirements.statement: The monitoring and response functions shall reach the controlled safe state within 100 ms of a detectable power-path fault.
functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Safe-state command and maintenance indication occur before the 100 ms fault-tolerant interval.
functional_requirements.operating_condition: Normal pumping with the health monitor active
functional_requirements.verification_method: Boundary fault injection with timestamped response capture

function_requirement_links.rationale: Monitoring latency contributes directly to the response-time budget.
System-response latency completes the response-time budget.

interfaces.name: Power-path health signal
interfaces.flow_description: A conditioned health indication enters the monitoring logic.

interfaces.name: Safe-state command
interfaces.flow_description: The controller removes pump command and annunciates maintenance.

items.label: Voltage-drop measurement
items.description: Observed electrical signature of contact degradation.

items.label: Timely safe-state command
items.description: Detected degradation produces a controlled response.

items.label: Diagnostic threshold and debounce
items.description: Calibrated logic controls sensitivity and response time.

items.label: Load transients and sensor tolerance
items.description: Expected variation can resemble or mask degradation.

items.label: Late or missed detection
items.description: The response occurs after the fault-tolerant interval or not at all.

failure_chains.detection_controls: Voltage-drop monitor with commanded safe-state response
failure_chains.severity_rationale: The unmitigated effect removes the required system function
failure_chains.frequency_rationale: Relevant design and field history support a very-low service frequency
failure_chains.monitoring_rationale: Fault-injection testing demonstrates response with remaining coverage limitations
failure_chains.system_response: Command controlled shutdown and annunciate maintenance
failure_chains.safe_state: Pump command removed with operator indication
failure_chains.mitigated_effect: Controlled loss of pump command with operator indication
failure_chains.remarks: Complete remaining boundary fault-injection tests before closure.

hraAtheana.efc: Misleading level indication under high workload

hraMermos.mission: Establish and maintain feed-and-bleed cooling within 30 minutes

scenarios.label: Crew mis-diagnoses the transient (leading CICA fails)
Procedure step omitted under time pressure
Failure to recover after wrong action

hraSparH.dependencyJustification: Same crew response shortly after the preceding human failure event.

blocks.content: This project combines traceable NIST datasets with representative engineering models across Perdura.
